nodes.speaker: Pip Marshwick
nodes.text: You picked a wet week to visit Lilypond. My cellar's half underwater and the masons swear it's not their levy.

altText.append: You brought proof to the council — Pip nods like you earned it.

choices.text: I already heard — that's why I'm here.
Not my problem.

nodes.speaker: Pip Marshwick
nodes.text: Good to meet you, {playerName}. Word travels fast on the causeway, then. Start with the cellar marks — you'll see the waterline yourself. Then see what Kess is building upriver.

altText.append: Sent to dig, were you? Good. The marks don't lie.
Someone upstream wanted this delivered to the right ears. You're here — that's the hard part.
You live close enough to smell the mold. I know that look.

nodes.speaker: Pip Marshwick
nodes.text: Suit yourself. The water remembers anyway.

nodes.speaker: Elder Shellen Domet
nodes.text: The Charter exists because water kills. We build walls so the Great Spill never repeats.

choices.text: Pip says the levy makes it worse downstream.
I need to see the plans.

nodes.speaker: Elder Shellen Domet
nodes.text: Your eyes are sharp. The downstream marks don't lie — examine the site yourself. I won't hide the diagrams.

nodes.speaker: Elder Shellen Domet
nodes.text: Frog merchants exaggerate. But... examine the site yourself. I won't hide the diagrams.

nodes.speaker: Elder Shellen Domet
nodes.text: Kess has the engineering scrolls at the levy camp. Tell him I sent you, if he'll listen.

nodes.speaker: Kess Ridge
nodes.text: Another frog sent you? The numbers don't lie. Thirty percent more south flow is a rounding error.

choices.text: The Charter allows fifteen percent.
What happened during the Great Spill?
[Frog] I smell upstream silt from here.
[Toad] Your levy poisons the shallows.
[Turtle] Slow down. Stone cracks when rushed.

nodes.speaker: Kess Ridge
nodes.text: Fine. Fifteen percent is the law. I'll... adjust the scrolls. Don't expect me to like it.

nodes.speaker: Kess Ridge
nodes.text: Charter was written when the river was different. Math doesn't care about sentiment.

nodes.speaker: Kess Ridge
nodes.text: Your nose works. Silt's from my cut channel — doesn't mean it's wrong.

nodes.speaker: Kess Ridge
nodes.text: Poison? Dramatic. Toads always blame the masons when their ponds shrink.

nodes.speaker: Kess Ridge
nodes.text: Cracks happen when frogs rush. I'm not rushing. I'm surviving.

nodes.speaker: Kess Ridge
nodes.text: ...I watched the water rise. I was small. I won't be small again.

nodes.speaker: Grizz Burrowman
nodes.text: Ferry's running. Depth marks on the post — I've kept them forty years.

choices.text: Can I check the marks?
Poachers at Blackfen?
These ferry tolls are robbery.
Jenna says the toll pays for levy stone hauls.

nodes.speaker: Grizz Burrowman
nodes.text: Doubled since the levy work started. Ask Jenna — she pays it every dawn run.

choices.text: I'll petition the council with you.
Understood.

nodes.speaker: Grizz Burrowman
nodes.text: You've got the voice for it. I'll draft the petition — you carry it to Mudwall.

nodes.speaker: Grizz Burrowman
nodes.text: Council won't listen to strangers. Ask Jenna first — she pays it every dawn run.

nodes.speaker: Grizz Burrowman
nodes.text: Good. Ask Jenna at the crossing — she knows the numbers.

nodes.speaker: Grizz Burrowman
nodes.text: Mudwall's fee, not mine. I'll petition the council — thanks for asking around.

nodes.speaker: Grizz Burrowman
nodes.text: Best at night when the ferry lamp's lit. Compare to last autumn's notch.

nodes.speaker: Grizz Burrowman
nodes.text: Toad and turtle thugs at the outlet. Harassing my nets. Try talking first — they're not worth your blood if they'll listen.

nodes.speaker: Marta Clayhollow
nodes.text: Tonics, poisons, same clay pot — dose makes the difference. What do you need?

choices.text: Something for poison saves.
Who benefits from the levy fight?
Pondwort's labels — red and green bands?

nodes.speaker: Marta Clayhollow
nodes.text: Red band tonic, green toxin. Pondwort swapped them on today's batch. Relabel before dusk — I'll supervise.

nodes.speaker: Marta Clayhollow
nodes.text: Clay phial, fifteen coin. Don't drink the whole thing.

nodes.speaker: Marta Clayhollow
nodes.text: Masons sell stone. Frogs sell fish. I sell to both and sleep in mud.

nodes.speaker: Jenna Leapwell
nodes.text: Courier's life — leap, deliver, repeat. Ferry tolls went up again.

choices.text: Grizz sent me about the toll.
Safe travels.

nodes.speaker: Jenna Leapwell
nodes.text: Council raised the crossing fee to pay for levy stone hauls. Grizz gets nothing extra — it's Mudwall's doing.

nodes.speaker: Jenna Leapwell
nodes.text: You too. Watch the mudwall cranes — they drop stones on frogs.

nodes.speaker: Tor Stoneback
nodes.text: Wall won't build itself. Kess works too fast but the stone's sound.

choices.text: Good luck.

nodes.speaker: Tor Stoneback
nodes.text: Luck's for frogs. We have mortar.

nodes.speaker: Fern Reedweaver
nodes.text: Reed mats, fish wraps — market's slow when cellars flood.

choices.text: Hope it dries.

nodes.speaker: Fern Reedweaver
nodes.text: Hope doesn't dry cellars. Councils do.

nodes.speaker: Old Myrtle Shellsong
nodes.text: The council records votes. I record who wished otherwise.

choices.text: Tell me about the fourth mural figure.
I'll leave you to your scrolls.

nodes.speaker: Old Myrtle Shellsong
nodes.text: Salamander folk. Erased when the Drainage failed. Names still in my ledger.

nodes.speaker: Croaker Finn
nodes.text: Fish! Five copper! Six if it's raining! Seven if Pip's watching!

choices.text: You're loud.

nodes.speaker: Croaker Finn
nodes.text: Loud sells fish!

nodes.speaker: Silt
nodes.text: Lantern's lit. Ferry's steady. Poachers at Blackfen if you're brave.

choices.text: Thanks.

nodes.speaker: Silt
nodes.text: Watch the depth post. Third notch from top — that's trouble.

nodes.speaker: Bramble
nodes.text: Causeway's clear. Crane almost dropped stone on a tadpole yesterday.

choices.text: I'll watch the masons.

nodes.speaker: Bramble
nodes.text: Good. Leap fast if you hear cables creak.

nodes.speaker: Pondwort
nodes.text: This one's tonic. Probably. Marta said check the label twice.

choices.text: You look worried. What happened?
Marta supervised the relabeling.
Good luck.

nodes.speaker: Pondwort
nodes.text: I think I swapped the red and green bands. Someone could drink toxin thinking it's tonic. Marta's shelf has the chart.

nodes.speaker: Pondwort
nodes.text: Red tonic, green toxin. I won't forget again. Probably.

nodes.speaker: Pondwort
nodes.text: I need it.

nodes.speaker: Elder Thatch
nodes.text: Chapel's half drowned but the murals endure. Examine the scratched figure if you dare.

choices.text: I will.

nodes.speaker: Elder Thatch
nodes.text: Water rises at dusk. So do old memories.

nodes.speaker: Rivulet
nodes.text: Quiet near the pool, please. Tadpoles startle easy.

choices.text: What do you teach them?
Sorry.

nodes.speaker: Rivulet
nodes.text: How to swim. How to share water. How the river remembers.

nodes.speaker: Sable Meadowrun
nodes.text: Mind the poultice cart, would you? Baskets tip easy on this causeway.

choices.text: What brings a vole to the basin?
Where do you sleep out here?
[Vole] Meadow burrows still flooding this spring?
Just passing through.

nodes.speaker: Sable Meadowrun
nodes.text: Meadow clans don't touch the levy fight — frog, toad, or shell. We just need somewhere dry to sell poultices. Basin's as good as any this side of the upland forest.

nodes.speaker: Sable Meadowrun
nodes.text: Lilypond reeds, mostly. Quieter than a burrow with six sisters in it, I'll say that much.

nodes.speaker: Sable Meadowrun
nodes.text: Ankle-deep by the third thaw, same as always. You'd know. Good to see another one of us out this far.

nodes.speaker: Sable Meadowrun
nodes.text: Mind the cart on your way out.

nodes.speaker: Fennick Farrow
nodes.text: Crossroads cart's the safest spot for a vole — equal walking distance from everyone's temper. Reeds and folk north, stone and shell east, smoke and tonics west, ferry water south.

choices.text: You're a long way from any burrow.
What are you selling?
[Vole] Any word from the upland trail?
Just passing through.

nodes.speaker: Fennick Farrow
nodes.text: Farther than most. Cart's been down every road between here and the Upland Trade Post twice this year. Basin folk fight over water. I just need somewhere flat to park it.

nodes.speaker: Fennick Farrow
nodes.text: Bit of everything, none of it much. Ask Marta in Croakend if you want a real potion — I mostly trade string, salt, and rumors.

nodes.speaker: Fennick Farrow
nodes.text: Trail's dry enough for the cart, if that's what you're asking. Meadow clans send word every full moon. Nothing about the levy fight, if that's what you're hoping.

nodes.speaker: Fennick Farrow
nodes.text: Mind the ruts. Cart's tipped twice already this week.

nodes.speaker: Elder Shellen Domet
nodes.text: The Council of Shells is assembled. You gathered evidence. Lilypond waits for your word.

choices.text: Expose Kess — the levy breaks the Charter.
Back the masons. Flood walls save lives.
Broker a compromise — modified levy + frog platforms.
Sell the evidence to whoever pays most.
Say nothing. Let them vote without you.

nodes.speaker: Council
nodes.text: Kess is halted. Masons glare. Pip weeps relief. The levy will be redrawn.

nodes.speaker: Council
nodes.text: The vote passes. Stone flows north. Lilymarket merchants exchange looks that promise a wet spring.

nodes.speaker: Council
nodes.text: Groans, then nods. A smaller levy. Floating platforms for the market. Myrtle writes your name in the ledger.

nodes.speaker: Council
nodes.text: You leave richer. Everyone knows. The river feels colder when you pass.

nodes.speaker: Council
nodes.text: Silence counts as consent here. Kess resumes work. The water will decide the rest.
